body.title: Igor background job: convert older chat logs into /home/akien/TheIgors/claude_chat_logs/
body.result: Registered StaleChatLogBackfiller push source to run export_chat.py --all hourly as background job
body.description:
Akien 2026-04-20: 'we should do a background job in igor to convert all the older stuff too if we have it?'

## Source discovery
Scan: ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-TheIgors/*.jsonl — every CC session transcript Claude has cached. These are dated by file mtime; newer sessions are the ones that would already be in /export-chat output.

## Conversion
For each transcript file:
- Derive date (from mtime or first message ts)
- Render the same way /export-chat does
- If claude_chat_logs/YYYYMMDD.md already exists, append a separator and the new content (don't overwrite)
- Skip transcripts already present (idempotent)

## Runs as Igor background source
Slot into push_sources.py, run at low priority during idle. Rate-limited so it doesn't flood /home/akien/TheIgors/claude_chat_logs/.

## Not in scope
- Indexing the chat logs into the graph — that's a bigger project
- Cross-machine transcripts (akiendell/yoga9i/yogai7) — future if needed

## Blocked by
- T-export-chat-skill (shares the render logic)

## ONE-SHOT BACKFILL DONE 2026-04-20 (CC, during day-close)

Ran export_chat.py --all once at day-close. 94 CC transcripts in ~/.claude/projects/-home-akien-TheIgors/ rendered to 23 dated files in claude_chat_logs/ (29MB total). Dir is gitignored — these files live as local recovery state, not source.

## Remaining scope = ongoing Igor background task only

This ticket narrows to the Igor-side part: register as a background push source (lab/claudecode/push_sources.py or equivalent) that runs export_chat.py --all at low priority during idle. Rate-limited to once per hour or once per idle-tick. Because --all is idempotent (appends with separator), running it repeatedly is safe — it only picks up newly-finished transcripts.

Note: one-shot made 2026-04-20.md redundant with the earlier /export-chat test run (same session content appears twice in that file with a separator). Minor polish follow-up if it matters: change --all behavior to write the union-per-day rather than simple append.
